articles.headline: Fourth World Cup still has South Korea captain Son Heung-Min feeling 'like a kid'
articles.description: South Korea captain Son Heung-min says he feels like a "kid" as he enters his fourth World Cup, where he will debut against the Czech Republic in a Group A match in Guadalajara on Thursday.

images.name: Son Heung-Min [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min was all smiles on Wednesday at South Korea's final training session before their 2026 FIFA World Cup opener against Czechia.
images.credit: Ulises RUIZ / AFP via Getty Images

articles.headline: Will 2026 prove to be Son Heung-Min's final FIFA World Cup hurrah?
articles.description: Son Heung-Min's previous three World Cups perfectly mirrored where he was in his career. So what does 2026 hold for him?

images.name: Will the 2026 World Cup be Son Heung-Min's last hurrah? [1500x844]
Son Heung-Min 2014 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min's first World Cup goal came in South Korea's ill-fated 2014 campaign -- where they picked up just one point from three games in their worst showing since 1998.
images.credit: ADRIAN DENNIS / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Son Heung-Min 2018 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min's memorable sealer in a stunning 2-0 win over Germany in 2018 ultimately proved in vain as South Korea narrowly failed to make it out of the group stage.

images.name: Son Heung-Min 2022 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: South Korea finally reached the round of 16 at the 2022 FIFA World Cup for the first time in 12 years -- although that was where their campaign would end with a 4-1 loss to Brazil.

images.name: Will Mexico or South Korea top Group A at the World Cup?
images.caption: Gab & Juls debate who will win Group A between Mexico, South Korea, Czechia and South Africa at the FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Japan have a plan to win the World Cup by 2050. How close will they get in 2026?
articles.description: Japan's "100 Year Vision" is well ahead of schedule, but are the Samurai Blue ready win the World Cup now?

images.name: Japan World Cup chances [1500x844]
images.alt: Wataru Endo, Takefusa Kubo and Ritsu Doan

images.name: Wataru Endo [1296x729]
images.caption: Although he was sidelined by injury in February, Wataru Endo has recovered in time for this third FIFA World Cup -- and first as captain of Japan.

images.name: Japan vs. Brazil [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan's preparations for the 2026 FIFA World Cup saw them claim friendly victories over Brazil and England for the first time ever.

images.name: Have injuries ruined Japan's chances of causing World Cup upsets?
images.caption: Gabe Tan reacts to Japan's squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: World Cup final squads ranked: Of all 48 national teams, who can win this summer?
articles.description: We have the final 26-player squads that can win the FIFA World Cup, so let's go through all 48 teams and rank where they stand.

images.name: Pochettino: USMNT are moving in a good direction ahead of World Cup
images.caption: Mauricio Pochettino speaks after the USMNT's 3-2 win over Senegal ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

categories.description: FIFA World Cup Qualifying - AFC/Concacaf Playoff

articles.headline: Midfield metronome Hwang In-Beom could be key for South Korea at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
articles.description: Hwang In-Beom is not South Korea's most-prominent name. But he could be their most important player at the World Cup.

images.name: Hwang In-Beom [1296x729]
images.caption: Although he was initially a doubt after picking up an injury in March, Hwang In-Beom has been passed fit to feature for South Korea at the 2026 FIFA World Cup -- where he will likely play a key role for them in midfield.

images.name: Hwang In-Beom [1296x729]
images.caption: Having had plenty of previous experience in Europe with Rubin Kazan, Olympiacos and Red Star Belgrade, Hwang In-Beom has now spent the past two seasons in the Eredivisie with Feyenoord.

images.name: Son Heung-min set for fourth World Cup with South Korea
images.caption: South Korea name their 26-man squad for the 2026 World Cup, headlined by a fourth competition appearance for captain Son Heung-min.

articles.headline: Why Japan could be the most exciting team at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
articles.description: Even with a couple of key players out through injury, Japan could still be the most exciting team at the 2026 World Cup.

images.name: Japan [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan's preparation for the 2026 FIFA World Cup has seen them record victories over Brazil and England for the first time ever over the past seven months.

images.name: Kaoru Mitoma [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan were dealt a real blow ahead of the World Cup after injuy ruled out Kaoru Mitoma, who scored the only goal of the game when they beat England 1-0 at Wembley back in March.

images.name: Have injuries ruined Japan's chances of causing World Cup upsets?
images.caption: Gabe Tan reacts to Japan's squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

article.headline: Goodwin brace against Lebanon edges Australia closer to World Cup
article.description: Craig Goodwin scored a brace on his return from illness as Australia booked their place in the third round of Asian World Cup qualifying with a 5-0 rout of Lebanon at Canberra Stadium on Tuesday.
article.linkText: Clinical Australia put 5 past Lebanon in WCQ rout

images.name: Craig Goodwin [1296x729]
images.caption: Australia's Craig Goodwin drills his shot past the Lebanon goal keeper.

peers.name: Craig Goodwin [1296x1296]
peers.caption: Australia's Craig Goodwin drills his shot past the Lebanon goal keeper.

peers.name: Craig Goodwin [1296x729]
peers.caption: Australia's Craig Goodwin drills his shot past the Lebanon goal keeper.

peers.name: Craig Goodwin [864x1296]
peers.caption: Australia's Craig Goodwin drills his shot past the Lebanon goal keeper.

peers.name: Craig Goodwin [1296x864]
peers.caption: Australia's Craig Goodwin drills his shot past the Lebanon goal keeper.

peers.name: Craig Goodwin [1296x518]
peers.caption: Australia's Craig Goodwin drills his shot past the Lebanon goal keeper.

images.name: Goodwin scores double in Socceroos' 5-0 win over Lebanon
images.caption: Craig Goodwin shows out in the Socceroos' dominant 5-0 win over Lebanon, contributing two assists and two goals.

article.story:
Australia are through to the final round of World Cup qualification after a masterful display from winger Craig Goodwin led them to a 5-0 win against Lebanon.

At Canberra's GIO Stadium, Goodwin scored two goals and created another two with gorgeous deliveries into the box to get the Socceroos the comfortable result.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

A dream night for Graham Arnold's side saw both Kusini Yengi and John Iredale net their first international goals as Australia continues to search for a long-term answer up front.

They are now guaranteed a berth in third-round qualifiers which start in September, where a top-two finish in a six-team group will carry them to the 2026 showpiece tournament.

It will be a sixth consecutive trip to the FIFA World Cup finals for the Socceroos if they qualify.

Forward Goodwin, who sat out last week's 2-0 win over the Lebanese while battling a virus, rocketed in a long-range strike after half-time and steered in the final goal in the 81st minute to the delight of a record crowd of 25,023 at the venue.

"Maybe I'll have to be sick more often," the Saudi-based winger joked after the game. "It turned out well, I had a good rest.

"I'd only had a couple of training sessions before the game but I spoke with the boss and felt good, so he chucked me in."

The Canberra crowd only had to wait two minutes for Australia to lead, centre-forward Yengi finding his goal courtesy of an inch-perfect curling cross from Goodwin.

Yengi was in the right spot but jabbed his close-range effort onto the post, before shinning home the rebound.

Goodwin's delivery into the box regularly tested the Lebanese defence, with Ajdin Hrustic skying an effort from the edge of the area after 30 minutes following one of the winger's most piercing balls.

Another teasing ball from the outside of his boot invited Harry Souttar to add to his impressive international tally, but the towering centre-half directed his close-range header onto the post.

But those wasted chances mattered little, Goodwin producing a teasing set-piece delivery that forced Lebanese defender Bassel Jradi to turn the ball into his own net just two minutes into the second half.

Soon after, Goodwin found space on the left wing and lashed a dipping volley past Lebanon keeper Mostafa Matar from well outside the box.

Arnold made four team tweaks, including Goodwin's inclusion up front, and used the second-half lead to give debuts to Patrick Yazbek and Josh Nisbet.

Yazbek made an early impact, whipping in a perfect cross that Iredale could easily turn home, before Goodwin found an easy back-post tap-in for his second on 81 minutes.

Five days on from a 2-0 win against the same opponent in Sydney, the Socceroos looked far more dangerous in attack and turned in a focused defensive effort that offered their opponents very little opportunity.

"I was very happy and proud of the young ones. They took ownership when they went on the park and the future is bright," coach Arnold said afterwards.

Lebanon entered the contest needing points to try and boost their own path to qualification, but spent much of the match defending with 10 players behind the ball and rarely ventured forward with many numbers.

Conceding early against the Socceroos for a second straight game, Lebanon could have equalised on 14 minutes but Nader Matar fired his shot into the side-netting.

Information from Reuters contributed to this report.

season.name: 2026 World Cup Qualifying - AFC, Second Round

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal!  Lebanon 0, Australia 1. Kusini Yengi (Australia) left footed shot from very close range to the bottom left corner.

Ali Tneich (Lebanon) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Australia. Patrick Yazbek replaces Ajdin Hrustic because of an injury.
Substitution, Lebanon. Karim Darwich replaces Hassan Maatouk.

Khalil Khamis (Lebanon) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, Australia. John Iredale replaces Kusini Yengi.

Sam Silvera (Australia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Second Half ends, Lebanon 0, Australia 5.
